nodes.goal: Load Chicago Loop OSM layers (surface, parks, water, roads, buildings) from PBF.
Run WGSL shadow-accumulation shader per road segment — sum minutes of shadow cast by one building over the June solstice (07:00–19:00).
Render the full layer stack in 3D with roads coloured by shadow minutes.

nodes.content: {"plot": {"dataRef": "table_osm_roads", "mark": "bar", "axis": ["shadow", "@transform"], "title": "Minutes of shadow per Chicago Loop road segment", "transform": {"preset": "binning-1d", "options": {"bins": 13}}, "events": ["brushX"]}}
nodes.goal: Show a brushable shadow-minutes histogram; brushing highlights roads on the 3D map.

dataflow.name: Autark GPU shader
dataflow.description: WebGPU compute via AutkGrammar: a per-road shadow-accumulation shader sums how many minutes of shadow one Chicago Loop building casts onto each road segment, hour by hour from 07:00 to 19:00 on the June solstice. Roads are coloured by the result and linked to a brushable histogram. The shader runs inside the grammar's compute block; the casting building's footprint is supplied as a uniform matrix.
